L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the buffer size calculation for encoding operations. When processing very large input data, the arithmetic used to compute the required output buffer size silently wraps around, resulting in an undersized buffer allocation. Subsequent unsafe writes to this buffer exceed its bounds, leading to memory corruption and potentially arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Base64 encoding output size calculation and buffer allocation logic
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the buffer size calculation for base64 encoding operations. When encoding very large input, the multiplication and addition used to compute the output buffer size silently wrap around due to unchecked arithmetic on the platform's native integer type, resulting in an undersized buffer allocation. Subsequent unsafe writes to this buffer exceed its bounds, leading to memory corruption and potentially arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The encoded_size function uses unchecked arithmetic (multiplication and addition) to calculate the output buffer size, which can silently overflow on large inputs, causing an undersized allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds writes via unsafe code.
